title: Decadent Smoky Norteño Chicken Burrito
description: A rich, creamy Mexican dish featuring smoky chicken, dried cranberries, and a blend of spices. Perfect for a post-workout meal or dinner with friends.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, cumin, and garlic powder.
Rub the spice mixture all over the chicken breasts and place on a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, mix together softened cream cheese, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and kosher salt.
Add shredded chicken to the cream cheese mixture and mix until well combined.
Warm the large flour tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the burritos by spreading a spoonful of the chicken and cream cheese mixture onto a tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of shredded cheese.
Add a few dried cranberries to the burrito, if desired.
Serve the burritos hot with a dollop of sour cream on top, if desired.
Cook time for the burritos is approximately 5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tortilla is warm and pliable.
Cut the burritos in half and serve immediately.
For an added crunch, top the burritos with chopped cilantro or sliced radishes, if desired.
